title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... its Insane
summary: Anthropic released UltraCode, a Claude feature that spins up up to 10 parallel agents managed by code instead of a master LLM. The tutorial covers parallel agent patterns, judge panels, adversarial verification, and deterministic fan-out for building agentic operating systems with optimal model selection via OpenRouter.
writeup: The video walks through UltraCode, a Claude feature from Anthropic that launches up to 10 parallel agents coordinated by code rather than a master LLM. It covers concrete orchestration patterns: parallel agent setups, judge panels, adversarial verification, and deterministic fan-out, with model selection routed through OpenRouter. The framing is building agentic operating systems, where code-driven control replaces a single overseeing model. The takeaways center on how deterministic coordination and panel-based verification shape multi-agent reliability.
